user_input: How SNAIC use AI?
reference_contexts: ['Strategic Pillars Pillar 1: AI Application Development for Industry SNAIC works with companies to co-develop practical AI solutions for real use cases. Pillar 2: AI Talent Development SNAIC develops AI talent through Industrial Doctorates, Industry Master’s programmes, undergraduate training, and upskilling for industry employees. Pillar 3: Translational AI Research SNAIC conducts applied research that bridges academic innovation and industry implementation. Collaboration Model Model Summary SNAIC follows a “1 (SIT) + 1 (NVIDIA) to many companies” model. Model Benefits End-to-end AI translation of impactful AI solutions Co-supervision by SIT and NVIDIA experts Increased AI adoption across industries AI competency development for students and working professionals Stronger AI talent pipeline Key Resources Resource Category: AI Talent SIT AI Researchers, including professors, research fellows, and engineers NVIDIA AI Researchers Postgraduate students, including Industry Doctorate and Industry Master’s students Undergraduate students Resource Category: Key Technologies Large Language Models (LLMs) Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) Multi-Modal Models Natural Language Processing (NLP) Computer Vision Speech Technology Resource Category: Compute Infrastructure DGX H200 Quantity: 4 systems Purpose: Large-scale model training and data processing Typical uses: LLM training, computer vision, multimodal AI, generative AI NVIDIA RTX A6000 Workstation Purpose: Development, testing, and running AI applications Typical uses: Model development, fine-tuning, inference, simulation, rendering AI Expertise Areas AI Foundations Deep Learning Machine Learning Continual Learning Predictive Analysis Physics-informed Neural Networks Generative AI Multimodal Foundation Models Large Language Models Vision Language Models Synthetic Data Generation Retrieval-Augmented Generation Agentic AI Personified AI Conversational AI Automation Agents Perception AI Computer Vision Speech Technology Natural Language Processing Sentiment Analysis AI for Digital Twins Domain Areas Healthcare and Biomedical Education and Learning Predictive and Generative Systems Human-Centered and Interactive Systems Industry and Manufacturing Secure and Sustainable Systems AI Technology Pillars LLMs RAG Multimodal AI NLP Speech AI Computer Vision Training and Talent Programmes Programme: IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme Goal: Train more than 200 fresh graduates and mid-career professionals over 3 years to become AI practitioners. Structure: 1. 2 months of intensive training in GenAI, LLMs, and Agentic AI. 2. 4 months of work on real-life projects with industry partners. Outcome: Participants gain current AI skills, practical project experience, and exposure to industry use cases. Programme: Applied AI Doctoral Training Centre Goal: Train 10 industrial doctorate students annually on complex, industry-driven challenges. Project type: Multi-year research projects that cannot be solved with off-the-shelf tools. Role in talent pathway: Creates a pathway from AI practitioner to applied AI researcher and innovator. Talent Pathway Summary The IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme and the Applied AI Doctoral Training Centre together form a tiered AI talent development pathway. Operating Areas Admin and Documentation Admin Documentation Guides People Operations Employee management for staff, stakeholders, students, assistants, and interns Onboarding Performance Offboarding and handover Recruitment Job description publishing CV retrieval Screening Interview Offer Hire Projects and Research Research Development Project management Grants Overseas university exchange and internships AI Centre Space and Infrastructure Renovation Lab safety Networking and internet access Resource access and maintenance Network DGX systems Collaboration Process with SNAIC Process Overview The collaboration process has 6 stages: 1. Pre-engagement questionnaire 2. 1-to-1 consultation 3. Project scoping 4. Resource identification 5. Project development 6. Project deployment or scaling Step 1: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire Step name: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire Purpose: Understand the company’s needs and objectives. What the company does: Fill in a questionnaire. What SNAIC does with the information: - Reviews the problem statement - Matches the problem statement to SNAIC capabilities - Assigns the right Technical Lead for the first consultation Output of this step: Initial understanding of needs and assigned Technical Lead. Step 2: 1-to-1 Consultation Step name: 1-to-1 Consultation Purpose: Explore whether AI can address the company’s problem statements. What happens in this step: - SNAIC and the company have an in-depth discussion - They identify where AI solutions can create significant value - They clarify possible use cases and expectations Output of this step: Initial alignment on business problem, AI opportunities, and expected value. Step 3: Project']
reference: SNAIC uses AI to co-develop practical industry solutions, train AI talent, and carry out applied research, supported by advanced AI technologies and strong compute resources.

Programme: IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme

Goal: Train more than 200 fresh graduates and mid-career professionals over 3 years to become AI practitioners.

Collaboration Process with SNAIC

Process Overview

The collaboration process has 6 stages: 1. Pre-engagement questionnaire 2. 1-to-1 consultation 3. Project scoping 4. Resource identification 5. Project development 6. Project deployment or scaling

Step 1: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire

Step name: Pre-Engagement Questionnaire

Purpose: Understand the company’s needs and objectives.

What the company does: Fill in a questionnaire.

What SNAIC does with the information: - Reviews the problem statement - Matches the problem statement to SNAIC capabilities - Assigns the right Technical Lead for the first consultation

Output of this step: Initial understanding of needs and assigned Technical Lead.

Step 2: 1-to-1 Consultation

Step name: 1-to-1 Consultation

Purpose: Explore whether AI can address the company’s problem statements.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC and the company have an in-depth discussion - They identify where AI solutions can create significant value - They clarify possible use cases and expectations

Output of this step: Initial alignment on business problem, AI opportunities, and expected value.

Step 3: Project Scoping

Step name: Project Scoping

Purpose: Define the roadmap for integrating AI into operations.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC AI experts work with the company - Project milestones are defined - Deliverables are defined - The AI adoption roadmap is outlined

Output of this step: Project roadmap, milestones, and deliverables.

Step 4: Resource Identification

Step name: Resource Identification

Purpose: Determine the resources, expertise, budget, and funding mechanism needed.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC and the company identify required talent, technology, and budget - Both parties align on a suitable funding mechanism - Both parties confirm resource contributions

Possible funding mechanisms: - Company-funded project - Joint grant application by company and SIT - Industry Doctorate or Industry Master’s project - IMDA 6-Month AI Training Programme - Integrative Team Project (ITP)

SNAIC resource contribution may include: - AI talent from SIT and NVIDIA - Postgraduate students - Undergraduate students - Key AI technologies - NVIDIA DGX H200 compute resources

Industry partner contribution may include: - Domain experts - Problem statements - Available data - Funding

Output of this step: Agreed resources, funding route, and role allocation.

Step 5: Project Development

Step name: Project Development

Purpose: Build and refine an AI solution.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC works closely with the company team - A minimum viable project (MVP) is developed - AI models are selected - AI models are customised and refined - The solution is tailored to the company’s needs and objectives

Output of this step: Working MVP and refined AI solution design.

Step 6: Project Deployment or Scaling

Step name: Project Deployment or Scaling

Purpose: Move the MVP into production or expand it.

What happens in this step: - SNAIC advances the MVP into a production-ready solution - The solution is deployed or scaled - SNAIC provides continuous support

Output of this step: Production-ready solution or scaled deployment with ongoing support.

Frequently Asked Questions

FAQ 1: Can a participant work and study during the IMDA AI Programme?

The communicated position is that participants may join if they take no-pay leave for 6 months. This avoids overlap between full-time salary, the IMDA incentive, and normal company work during training.

FAQ 2: Can a participant use SkillsFuture or other government subsidies for the IMDA AI Programme?

No. The programme is already funded by IMDA. Other government subsidies should not be used because that would amount to double dipping.']
